metrics.name: BoJ
metrics.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

simulation_result.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.875 (counted n=3); simulation-conditional 0.0 (resolved mass 1.00); headline 0.875 via combiner_unavailable_range_only. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
bounded omitted-state residual 0.631: the interval widens to [0.0, 0.6314] — private-state omissions are BOUNDS, never unknown-state worlds

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the mass-based recovery blend (resolved mass keeps its simulated conditional; unresolved mass takes the grounded prior); the two inputs are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule

blueprint.causal_thesis: The BOJ Policy Board votes on a proposal to raise the uncollateralized overnight call rate from 0.75% to 1.0%. A simple majority of the 9 members is required. The outcome hinges on whether the three April dissenters (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura) are joined by at least two more members, or whether Gover

terminal_canonicalization.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

boj_gradual_hiking_cycle.claim: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.

provenance.definition: The Bank of Japan is in a gradual tightening cycle, raising rates in small increments after ending negative rates in March 2024.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: BOJ raised policy rate from -0.1% to 0-0.1% in March 2024, then to 0.25% in July 2024, then to 0.5% in January 2025, then to 0.75% in March 2025.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

dissenting_pressure_for_hike.claim: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.

provenance.definition: Multiple board members have consistently dissented in favor of a rate hike, indicating internal pressure for tightening.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, three members proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but were defeated 6-3.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

market_expectations_elevated.claim: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.

provenance.definition: Market participants and economists widely expect a rate hike at the June meeting, with Polymarket probability at 63%.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Reuters reported that the BOJ has 'locked in' signals for a June rate hike, and nearly two-thirds of economists expect 1.0% by end-June.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan has locked in signals for a June rate hike, with nearly two-thirds of economists expecting the rate to reach 1.0% by end-June.

nakagawa_junko.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Nakagawa Junko votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Nakagawa Junko dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where she supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Nakagawa Junko proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

takata_hajime.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Takata Hajime votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Takata Hajime dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where he supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Takata Hajime proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

tamura_naoki.quantity: this member dissents on a rate hold

provenance.definition: Tamura Naoki votes against maintaining the current rate and proposes a higher rate.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Tamura Naoki dissents from a decision to keep the rate unchanged.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes where he supports a hold or is absent.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Tamura Naoki proposed raising the rate to 1.0% and dissented from the hold decision.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

ueda_kazuo.quantity: this governor votes with the majority on rate decisions

provenance.definition: Governor Ueda votes in favor of the final decision, whether to hold or raise.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where Ueda supports the majority decision.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the April 27-28, 2026 meeting, Governor Ueda voted with the majority to keep the rate unchanged at 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Policy Board voted 6-3 to keep the rate unchanged.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting

provenance.definition: The BOJ announces a guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate higher than the current 0.75%.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any increase in the rate, e.g., to 1.0% or higher.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At the March 18-19, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.5% to 0.75%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.75%.

cases.description: At the January 23-24, 2025 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0.25% to 0.5%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.5%.

cases.description: At the July 30-31, 2024 meeting, the BOJ raised the rate from 0-0.1% to 0.25%.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank of Japan decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to approximately 0.25%.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

reversal_search.why: every actor already holds a reversal-capable state

other_members.residual_provenance: uncovered feasible option(s) ['maintain at 0.75%', 'raise to 1.0%'] with no counted cases — bounded per option

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda believes the BOJ must maintain a gradual hiking cycle to avoid disrupting fragile economic recovery, and that insufficient data by June (especially wage and consumption figures) precludes a hike. He prioritizes consensus-building and fears repeating the premature tightening mistakes of the 2000
beliefs: Gradual normalization is essential to avoid market disruption
Data dependency requires more evidence on wage-price spiral sustainability
goals: Maintain BOJ credibility as a cautious, predictable institution
Avoid policy reversal that would damage confidence
commitments: Publicly stated commitment to data-dependent approach
Previous votes to hold rate at 0.75%
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Internal dissent from three members, market expectations for hike, but also political pressure to avoid choking growth

relationships.other_members: seeks to maintain majority support for hold

stances: Cautious
Data-dependent
Consensus-oriented
supporting_evidence_ids: Ueda's past statements on gradual normalization
contradicting_evidence_ids: Reuters report of 'locked in' signals for June hike
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not publicly signaled a June hike
He emphasized data dependency in recent press conferences
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote to hold rate at 0.75%, argue that more data is needed, and attempt to persuade dissenting members to wait
assumptions: Wage data for May will be ambiguous

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda privately concludes that the risk of falling behind the curve (yen weakness, imported inflation) outweighs the risk of hiking prematurely. He decides to support a 25bp hike to 1.0% to maintain BOJ credibility and align with market expectations, despite his personal preference for caution.
beliefs: Inaction could trigger yen crisis and imported inflation spiral
Market expectations are now a factor that must be managed
goals: Prevent loss of BOJ control over yield curve
Signal that BOJ is not behind the curve
commitments: Previous statements that BOJ will act if inflation expectations overshoot
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Strong market expectations (63% implied probability), dissenting members' persistence, yen depreciation

relationships.nakagawa_junko: aligns with her on need for hike
relationships.other_members: works to build majority for hike

stances: Pragmatic
Market-aware
Crisis-averse
historical_case_refs: BOJ 2006 rate hike after prolonged zero rates
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not explicitly ruled out June hike
He has acknowledged yen weakness as a risk
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote for 25bp hike to 1.0%, issue statement emphasizing data dependency and optionality for future moves
assumptions: May CPI data shows sticky inflation

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa remains convinced that the BOJ must hike to 1.0% to prevent inflation from becoming entrenched and to normalize policy. She views the April hold as a missed opportunity and will again propose a hike, possibly with stronger arguments about wage data and yen weakness.
beliefs: Inflation is above target and requires immediate action
Delaying hikes risks asset bubbles and yen crisis
goals: Achieve rate hike to 1.0% at June meeting
Shift BOJ policy stance toward normalization
commitments: Public statements on need for normalization
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations support her view, but she faces majority opposition
stances: Hawkish
Urgent
Inflation-focused
distinguishing_observations: She has consistently voted for hike
She may cite recent wage data if strong
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Propose 25bp hike to 1.0%, argue that data supports action, and attempt to sway other members

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that a global recession is imminent, making further rate hikes too risky despite domestic inflation persistence.
beliefs: Global economic indicators (e.g., US slowdown, European energy crisis) signal a sharp downturn within 6 months.
Raising rates now would exacerbate Japan's export vulnerability and risk deflationary relapse.
The BOJ's gradual hiking cycle must pause to preserve financial stability.
goals: Avoid triggering a recession by overtightening.
Maintain policy flexibility to cut rates if needed.
Prevent yen carry trade unwinding from destabilizing markets.
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are elevated, but her private fear of recession outweighs external pressure.
stances: Opposes any rate hike above 0.75% at this meeting.
Prefers to hold and wait for clearer global signals.
May dissent against the majority if they push for a hike.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2008 rate cut after Lehman shock despite earlier tightening bias.
distinguishing_observations: She has not publicly voiced recession fears, but her private briefings show concern about global trade data.
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Vote to keep the rate at 0.75% and issue a statement emphasizing downside risks to global growth.

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that failing to hike now would permanently damage the BOJ's inflation-fighting credibility, even if data is mixed.
beliefs: Markets interpret any hold as capitulation to dovish pressure, risking yen depreciation and imported inflation.
The 6-3 vote in April showed strong internal support for a hike; not delivering now would signal weakness.
Credibility is more important than short-term economic smoothing.
goals: Establish the BOJ as a credible inflation fighter.
Prevent yen from weakening further, which would raise import costs.
Deliver on the implicit promise of normalization.
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Market expectations are elevated; failing to hike would disappoint and trigger volatility.
stances: Strongly supports raising the rate to 1.0% or above 0.75%.
Will actively persuade other members to join her.
Views dissent as necessary if the majority holds.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2000 rate hike decision (Mieno's 'credibility' argument) that was later reversed.
distinguishing_observations: She has been a persistent hawk; this state intensifies that stance with a focus on institutional reputation.
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Propose a hike to 1.0% and vote for it; if defeated, issue a strong dissenting opinion citing credibility risks.

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa Junko privately believes that the government's fiscal expansion will overheat the economy, requiring a preemptive rate hike to prevent asset bubbles.
beliefs: The government's new stimulus package (announced May 2026) will boost demand beyond capacity.
Low rates are fueling excessive risk-taking in real estate and equities.
Waiting for clearer inflation data risks a bubble burst.
goals: Preempt financial instability from asset bubbles.
Force the government to coordinate fiscal and monetary policy.
Protect the BOJ's independence by acting before fiscal pressures mount.
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Fiscal expansion creates political pressure to keep rates low, but she sees this as precisely the risk.
stances: Advocates for a hike to 1.0% or higher.
Argues that delay will require sharper tightening later.
Seeks to build a coalition with Takata and Tamura.
historical_case_refs: Japan's 1980s asset bubble partly blamed on delayed tightening despite fiscal stimulus.
distinguishing_observations: She has privately cited rising Tokyo office prices and margin trading volumes as warning signs.
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Vote for a hike to 1.0% and publicly cite financial stability risks in her statement.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata maintains his view that the BOJ should hike to 1.0% to preempt inflation risks and normalize policy. He is likely to again propose a hike, possibly with a modified proposal if he sees a chance to win over moderates.
beliefs: Inflation expectations are rising and need to be anchored
BOJ should not fall behind the curve
goals: Secure rate hike to 1.0%
Demonstrate commitment to price stability
stances: Hawkish
Proactive
Inflation-wary
distinguishing_observations: He may adjust proposal to 0.5bp if he sees no majority for 1.0%
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Propose hike to 1.0%, or possibly a compromise 0.5bp hike if needed to build majority

takata_hajime.claim: Takata believes that raising rates above 0.75% now risks triggering a global financial contagion due to hidden vulnerabilities in the banking system, making him prioritize financial stability over inflation control.
beliefs: A rate hike above 0.75% could expose leveraged positions in global bond markets, leading to a crisis similar to the 2023 US regional banking turmoil.
The BOJ's gradual hiking cycle must pause to assess spillover effects from recent US and European banking stress.
goals: Avoid a systemic financial crisis that would undermine the BOJ's credibility.
Maintain Japan's financial stability as a safe haven.
takata_hajime.pressures: Intense scrutiny from international financial institutions and domestic banks warning of liquidity strains.
stances: Vote to keep the rate at 0.75% or lower, opposing any hike to 1.0%.
Advocate for a data-dependent pause, citing global financial conditions.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2000 rate hike reversal due to deflationary shock
distinguishing_observations: Takata privately cites confidential BOJ stress tests showing bank exposure to foreign rate shocks.
He references the 2023 Silicon Valley Bank collapse as a cautionary tale.
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Takata would vote against any rate hike above 0.75% and lobby other members to hold steady, potentially joining the majority to keep rates unchanged.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata sees a once-in-a-decade opportunity to decisively break Japan's deflationary mindset by hiking to 1.0%, believing that hesitation now would permanently damage the BOJ's credibility and allow inflation expectations to re-anchor.
beliefs: Japan's economy has finally escaped the low-inflation trap, and a bold hike is needed to cement this shift.
Market expectations are already priced for a hike; failing to deliver would trigger yen depreciation and imported inflation.
goals: Establish the BOJ as a credible inflation-fighting central bank on par with the Fed or ECB.
Prevent a resurgence of deflationary psychology that has plagued Japan for decades.
takata_hajime.pressures: Strong support from export-oriented industries and foreign investors demanding yen strength.
stances: Vote for a hike to 1.0% and push for a more aggressive tightening path.
Argue that the gradual hiking cycle must accelerate to avoid falling behind the curve.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 2006-2008 rate hikes that successfully normalized policy
Fed's 2022 aggressive tightening to regain credibility
distinguishing_observations: Takata has privately argued that the BOJ's 0.75% rate is still 'emergency level' and must be normalized.
He points to rising wage growth and service prices as evidence of a virtuous cycle.
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Takata would lead the dissenting faction, proposing a hike to 1.0% and attempting to sway undecided members with data showing inflation persistence.

takata_hajime.claim: Takata believes that the government is about to announce a major fiscal expansion that would overheat the economy, so he must hike now to preemptively offset inflationary pressures and maintain BOJ independence.
beliefs: The Prime Minister's upcoming stimulus package will boost demand beyond capacity, requiring tighter monetary policy to prevent overheating.
Delaying a hike would force the BOJ to act more abruptly later, risking a policy mistake.
goals: Assert the BOJ's independence from political pressure by acting preemptively.
Coordinate monetary and fiscal policy to avoid an inflation spike.
takata_hajime.pressures: Leaked government budget plans and private briefings from Ministry of Finance officials.
stances: Vote for a hike to 1.0% as a preemptive measure against fiscal expansion.
Publicly emphasize the need for policy coordination without subordination.
historical_case_refs: BOJ's 1990 rate hikes to counter asset bubble fueled by fiscal policy
ECB's 2011 rate hike that was later reversed due to fiscal austerity
distinguishing_observations: Takata has met privately with LDP lawmakers who signaled a large supplementary budget.
He references the 1970s oil shock where delayed BOJ action led to stagflation.
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Takata would vote for a hike to 1.0% and argue that the BOJ must act now to maintain credibility, even if it means dissenting from the majority.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura continues to advocate for a hike to 1.0%, believing that the BOJ must act decisively to prevent inflation from overshooting. He is unlikely to change his stance unless data dramatically weakens.
beliefs: Current 0.75% rate is too accommodative
BOJ must act now to avoid sharper tightening later
goals: Achieve rate hike to 1.0%
Signal BOJ commitment to inflation target
tamura_naoki.pressures: Minority position, but market expectations support his view
stances: Hawkish
Decisive
Inflation-focused
distinguishing_observations: He may be open to compromise if data weakens
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Propose hike to 1.0%, argue that waiting is riskier
transition_triggers: Weak GDP data could soften his stance

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura believes that after three consecutive dissents for a hike to 1.0%, his credibility as a hawkish policy maker is on the line. He privately fears that if he does not secure a hike at this meeting, markets and colleagues will view him as ineffectual, weakening his influence on future policy. He
beliefs: Repeated dissents without eventual policy change erode a board member's credibility and market impact.
The April 2026 vote (6-3 against his proposal) showed he has support from Nakagawa and Takata, but needs to convert that into a majority.
If he fails again in June, his future dissents will be discounted by markets and other board members.
goals: Secure a rate hike to above 0.75% at this meeting to validate his hawkish stance.
Maintain his reputation as a credible inflation fighter.
Avoid being marginalized within the Policy Board.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Intense personal pressure to convert his repeated dissents into actual policy change, lest he lose influence and credibility.
stances: Strongly favors raising the overnight call rate to at least 1.0%.
Opposes any delay or further wait-and-see approach.
supporting_evidence_ids: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated
historical_case_refs: Similar pattern seen with former BOJ board member Takahide Kiuchi, who dissented multiple times before eventually being
distinguishing_observations: Tamura has been a consistent dissenter for a hike, suggesting a pattern of behavior that he may feel compelled to break
The April vote was close (6-3), so a shift of just one or two votes could change the outcome.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura will intensify private lobbying of swing voters (e.g., Ueda or other members) before the meeting, emphasizing the need to act now to preserve the board's credibility and avoid falling behind th

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura privately believes that the global economic outlook has deteriorated significantly since the April meeting, with new data showing slowing growth in key trading partners and rising financial instability. He fears that hiking now could trigger a sharp yen appreciation and export collapse, worse
beliefs: Recent global economic data (e.g., US slowdown, European recession risks) pose a serious downside risk to Japan's export-dependent recovery.
A rate hike above 0.75% could strengthen the yen too rapidly, hurting exporters and corporate profits.
The BOJ should prioritize financial stability and growth over inflation targeting in the current uncertain global environment.
goals: Avoid a policy mistake that could tip Japan into recession.
Maintain flexibility to respond to global shocks.
Preserve the BOJ's credibility as a prudent, data-dependent central bank.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Conflicting pressures: his hawkish reputation vs. genuine concern about global contagion; he may face criticism from allies for appearing to flip.
stances: Prefers to hold the rate at 0.75% or lower until global outlook clarifies.
Opposes a hike at this meeting due to external risks.
supporting_evidence_ids: Shared world conditions: boj_gradual_hiking_cycle, market_expectations_elevated (but these could be reinterpreted as risks)
contradicting_evidence_ids: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%
historical_case_refs: In 2008, the BOJ held rates steady during the global financial crisis despite earlier hawkish signals, citing external r
distinguishing_observations: Tamura's previous dissents were based on domestic inflation concerns; a shift to global risk focus would be a notable ch
The April vote was close, so his defection from the hawkish camp could swing the outcome to a hold.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura will privately communicate his concerns to Governor Ueda and other key members, arguing for a pause. He may also propose a statement that emphasizes downside risks to the economy, signaling a m

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura privately believes that a full hike to 1.0% is not achievable at this meeting due to Ueda's strong opposition and the risk of a market overreaction. Instead, he aims to secure a compromise: a smaller hike to 0.85% or 0.90%, coupled with a hawkish forward guidance signaling further hikes. He s
beliefs: A 1.0% hike is ideal but politically impossible now; a smaller hike is better than no hike.
Ueda may accept a modest increase if it is framed as a 'measured step' rather than a full shift.
A compromise would demonstrate the board's ability to act while preserving cohesion.
goals: Achieve a rate increase above 0.75% to show progress on normalization.
Avoid a repeat of the April defeat, which could weaken the hawkish faction.
Set the stage for further hikes in subsequent meetings.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Pressure from both hawks (who want 1.0%) and doves (who want no hike); must navigate between them to find a middle ground.
stances: Supports a rate hike, but is flexible on the exact level.
Willing to accept a smaller increase if it secures majority support.
supporting_evidence_ids: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%
historical_case_refs: In 2000, the BOJ raised rates by 25bp to 0.25% as a compromise between hawks and doves, avoiding a larger move.
distinguishing_observations: Tamura's previous proposals were for 1.0%, so a shift to a smaller hike would signal pragmatism.
The April vote was 6-3 against 1.0%, but a smaller hike might attract one or two swing voters.
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Tamura will privately propose a compromise rate (e.g., 0.85% or 0.90%) to Ueda and other members, arguing it is a 'safe' step that addresses inflation concerns without shocking markets. He will try to

other_members.claim: The other five members (who voted with Ueda in April) remain cautious, believing that more data is needed before hiking. They are influenced by Ueda's leadership and concerns about economic fragility.
beliefs: Gradual normalization is appropriate
Data dependency requires patience
goals: Maintain policy stability
Avoid premature tightening
other_members.pressures: Market expectations, but also political pressure to support growth
stances: Cautious
Data-dependent
Consensus-oriented
distinguishing_observations: They have not publicly signaled a change in stance
other_members.action_if_state: Vote to hold rate at 0.75%, supporting Ueda's cautious approach
transition_triggers: Strong wage data could shift some to favor hike

other_members.claim: Some of the other members become convinced by market pressures, yen weakness, or strong data that a hike is necessary. They may shift to support a 25bp hike, potentially creating a majority for action.
beliefs: Risk of inaction now outweighs risk of action
Market expectations must be managed
goals: Prevent loss of BOJ credibility
Align policy with economic reality
commitments: Previous hold votes, but open to new data
other_members.pressures: Market expectations (63% implied probability), yen depreciation, dissenting members' arguments
stances: Pragmatic
Market-aware
Data-responsive
distinguishing_observations: They may be swayed by strong May data or yen crisis
other_members.action_if_state: Vote for 25bp hike to 1.0%, joining dissenting members to form majority
assumptions: May data shows strong wages and inflation

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect the vote count; quorum must be met.

checks.note: all terminal-writing effects are known mechanical kinds
at least one resolving path exists
vote tally in members — exact units
synthetic YES→1 / NO→0 through the live path

record.why: vote terminals read the tally directly

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: The trajectory of global growth and its impact on Japan's export sector is still unclear.
deliberations.note: The global growth trajectory remains highly uncertain, with US slowdown and European recession risks intensifying. A rate hike now could trigger yen appreciation, harming exporters and risking recessi

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: I am uncertain whether a vote for 1.0% would gain majority support or lead to a deadlock, given the doves' resistance.
deliberations.note: I recognize that a 1.0% vote risks deadlock and could weaken the hawkish faction if defeated again. A compromise at 0.75% is more likely to gain majority support, especially if framed as a measured st

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: The exact state of global bond market leverage and spillover effects from US and European banking stress remains unclear.
deliberations.note: The tradeoff is between preventing a potential systemic crisis by keeping rates low versus risking inflation or yen depreciation by not hiking. Global bond market leverage is opaque, but US and Europe

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: The exact timing and severity of the global downturn remain uncertain.
deliberations.note: The global downturn is uncertain in timing and severity, but my private fear of recession and the risk of yen carry trade unwinding outweigh the market pressure for a hike. Holding at 0.75% preserves

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether I have enough support from other members to pass the hike.
deliberations.note: I have only two confirmed allies (Takata and Tamura) out of nine members, so a vote now would almost certainly fail. A failed vote would weaken my credibility and make future hikes harder. However, de

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the wage-price spiral is sustainable enough to justify a hike without triggering a policy reversal.
deliberations.note: I weigh the risk of a premature hike against the risk of falling behind the curve. The three hawkish members push for a hike, but the data on wage-price sustainability remains inconclusive. A hike now

coalescer.randomness_rule: content-addressed (state/context-seeded) draws — equal keys share the future transition law by construction; independent streams carry a tag that refuses the merge

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine); represented states carry the full branch mass — omitted-state residuals are bounded interval-wideners, never unknown-state worlds

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: behavioral replicates not requested (deterministic execution: content-addressed draws make replicate 0 exhaustive)

provenance.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 15-16, 2026 meeting
provenance.fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

notes: no leakage-audited reliability combiner is fitted — prior and simulation are reported separately with the feasible combined range; no fixed blend is applied and Lean V2 must not become default on this basis

consequences.boundary_contract: delivery mechanics precompiled; interpretation/persuasion/reaction remain branch-local actor decisions

vote_on_rate.description: Each Policy Board member casts vote on proposed rate.
